slides.title: Supabase Masterclass
slides.subtitle:
The Open-Source Firebase Alternative
A Deep Dive into Architecture, Database, Auth, Realtime, and CLI Workflows

slides.name: Aravind Govindhasamy
slides.title: Software Developer
slides.oss_experience: Backend Development, Database Design & Automation Specialist

slides.title: Introduction to Supabase & The BaaS Revolution
slides.subtitle: Understanding Backend-as-a-Service and Open-Source Philosophy

slides.title: The Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS) Paradigm
slides.caption: Accelerating modern web and mobile development
slides.content:
🚀 Backend-as-a-Service Revolution

BaaS allows developers to focus entirely on frontend experiences by outsourcing database management, authentication, storage, and serverless logic to a managed or self-hosted platform.

  • ⚡ Zero Boilerplate - Skip server initialization, routing, and environment configurations.
  • 🔒 Secure by Default - Built-in security layers like JSON Web Tokens (JWT) and database level security.
  • 📈 Auto-Scaling - Pay-as-you-grow compute power without infrastructure upkeep.

slides.title: Supabase vs. Google Firebase
leftPoints: 💾 Standard Relational SQL schema with foreign keys
🔒 Row Level Security (RLS) policies at the DB level
🚀 Complex joins and aggregations run instantly
rightPoints: 🛡️ Firebase Security Rules written in proprietary language
🐌 Complex joins require manual nested client queries
💸 Usage-based pricing (can lead to unexpected bills)

note.text: Supabase delivers standard SQL capabilities with BaaS convenience, removing vendor lock-in.
Kong routes client requests to dedicated GoTrue, PostgREST, and Realtime endpoints.

box.title: 🧩 No Custom Wrappers - Only Battle-Tested Open Source Tools:
list: PostgreSQL: The backbone of the entire system. You have full database access.
PostgREST: Inspects your schema and outputs a high-performance REST API.
GoTrue: An API written in Go for managing users and generating JWTs.
Phoenix: An Elixir framework utilizing WebSockets for real-time messages.

note.text: This modular composition allows Supabase to remain fully open source and highly scalable.

box.title: 🎨 Managing Database Resources Visually:
list: Table Editor: A spreadsheet-like interface for creating columns, editing data, and linking tables.
SQL Editor: A built-in scratchpad to execute complex SQL queries and analyze performance.
Auth Manager: Interface to inspect registered users, revoke sessions, and configure OAuth.
Database Metrics: Real-time charts detailing CPU usage, database size, and active connections.

slides.title: PostgreSQL: The Database Engine
slides.subtitle: Tapping into the Power of Relational Data Systems

slides.title: Why PostgreSQL?
slides.imageAlt: PostgreSQL Database logo and visual layout
slides.caption: The world's most advanced open-source database
slides.content:
🐘 PostgreSQL Core Strengths

Unlike other BaaS platforms that invent proprietary databases, Supabase gives you a raw, dedicated PostgreSQL engine with zero compromises.

  • 🔒 Full ACID Compliance - Guaranteeing transactional safety and database consistency.
  • 🛡️ Schemas & Constraints - Enforce data integrity with strong typing, foreign keys, and unique checks.
  • 🔌 Direct Access - Connect using any language-specific Postgres driver (pg, psycopg2, pq) or client tool.

box.title: 🏗️ Designing a relational schema:
list: Use standard foreign keys (`REFERENCES`) to establish relationships between tables.
Implement constraints like `NOT NULL`, `UNIQUE`, and `CHECK` to enforce business rules.
Enable database indexes (`CREATE INDEX`) to keep query lookups fast as tables grow.
Set cascade behaviors (`ON DELETE CASCADE`) to automatically manage orphaned children.

box.title: Designing profiles with a foreign key back to the user catalog:
box.code:
CREATE TABLE profiles (
  id UUID REFERENCES auth.users ON DELETE CASCADE PRIMARY KEY,
  username TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  avatar_url TEXT,
  points INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK (points >= 0),
  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT timezone('utc'::text, now()) NOT NULL
);

box.title: 🔌 Enable custom functionality directly inside the database:
list: pg_vector: Store vector embeddings and calculate distances for AI matching.
pg_cron: Set cron-like schedules to run SQL jobs inside the database.
pg_net: Make asynchronous HTTP web requests directly from Postgres triggers.
PostGIS: Manipulate geo-coordinates, polygons, and spatial indices.

box.title: Creating a custom procedure that clients can trigger:

note.text: SECURITY DEFINER causes the function to run with permissions of the creator (superuser), bypassing RLS.

box.title: ⚡ Trigger automatic actions based on database operations:
list: Triggers run automatically on INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE operations.
Enable workflows like auditing, calculating values, or calling external endpoints.
Can be configured to run BEFORE or AFTER the target SQL operation.

slides.title: Row Level Security (RLS)
slides.subtitle: Enforcing Authorization Rules at the SQL Layer

slides.title: What is Row Level Security?
slides.imageAlt: Security shield and data lock visualization
slides.caption: Securing data directly at the source
slides.content:
🛡️ Database-Level Access Control

Instead of writing API route middleware in Node.js or Python, Supabase relies on Row Level Security (RLS). All data queries are filtered based on SQL policies, regardless of how they are queried.

  • 🔒 Secure by Default - Once RLS is enabled, all access is blocked until explicit permissions are granted.
  • ⚙️ Postgres Evaluation - The DB engine checks each row before returning it.
  • 👥 Flexible Rules - Write separate policies for SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE.

box.title: Declaring security policies on the profiles table:

slides.title: RLS Request Lifecycle
slides.content:
Client Request (reads public.profiles)
       │
       ▼
 Kong API Gateway (validates API key)
       │
       ▼
 PostgREST Engine (extracts user's JWT token)
       │
       ▼
 PostgreSQL (maps JWT user_id to session parameter)
       │
       ├─► Is Row Level Security (RLS) enabled?
       │     ├─► NO  ──► Execute raw query (return all rows)
       │     └─► YES ──► Filter rows matching policies
       │                   │
       │                   ├─► Owner policy (auth.uid() = id) ──► Match!
       │                   └─► Admin policy (is_admin = true) ──► Match!
       ▼
Filtered Rows Returned to Client

note.text: Data filtering happens inside the database query plan, keeping RLS extremely efficient.

box.title: 🔗 How user identity maps to database policies:
list: Every request carries a JSON Web Token (JWT) representing the logged-in user.
PostgREST extracts this token and sets the transaction parameter `request.jwt.claims`.
The custom helper `auth.uid()` evaluates to the `sub` claim in that JWT.
Enables simple rules like `auth.uid() = user_id` to establish row ownership.

slides.title: Service Role Key vs. Anon Key
leftPoints: 🌐 Publicly exposed in client JS files
👥 Identifies queries as anonymous or user-authenticated
rightPoints: 🔑 Must NEVER be exposed in the browser
🛠️ Used for migrations, background jobs, and seed files

note.text: Never expose your service role key in frontend repositories or client applications!

box.title: 💡 Designing secure database policies:
list: Avoid recursions: Don't read table A inside table A's policy without using helper functions.
Test policies: Write unit tests or verify policies locally with different user contexts.
Specify operations: Avoid `FOR ALL` policies; write distinct rules for SELECT, INSERT, etc.
Use indexes: Ensure columns used in RLS policies (e.g. `owner_id`, `org_id`) are indexed.

slides.title: Authentication & User Management
slides.subtitle: Identity management, session handling, and social integrations

slides.title: GoTrue Identity Engine
slides.caption: Modern, secure user accounts out of the box
slides.content:
🔐 Comprehensive Auth Management

Supabase Auth is powered by GoTrue, an open-source API for managing users and issuing JSON Web Tokens (JWT).

  • 🔄 Secure Sessions - Supports access and refresh token rotation with customizable expiry.
  • 📧 Verification Flows - Automatic signup emails, email confirmation, and password reset templates.
  • 🔒 Encryption - Secure password hashing using bcrypt or argon2 database routines.

box.title: Registering a new user with custom meta-data:
Triggering social sign-in with Google, GitHub, or Apple:
📱 Modern authentication strategies:
list: Magic Links: Send a single-use login link via email to log users in immediately.
SMS / WhatsApp OTP: Send a 6-digit verification code to mobile numbers using providers like Twilio.
Passkeys: Log users in using biometrics (face ID, fingerprint) or hardware keys (WebAuthn).

box.title: Postgres trigger to duplicate auth users into public profiles:
👑 Implementing Role-Based Access Control (RBAC):
list: Customize user JWT claims via auth triggers or database app metadata.
Expose user roles (e.g. `admin`, `moderator`) directly inside the JWT payload.
Read these custom claims inside RLS policies using `auth.jwt() ->> 'role'`.
Restricts unauthorized API calls before they reach the database server.

slides.title: Auto-generated REST and GraphQL APIs
slides.subtitle: Instant Query Engines that Map Directly to Postgres

slides.title: How PostgREST Works
slides.imageAlt: Web servers and API conceptual design
slides.caption: Instant, sub-millisecond API endpoints
slides.content:
⚡ High-Speed REST Engine

PostgREST acts as a gateway that parses incoming RESTful URLs, converts them directly into a single SQL statement, and executes it inside PostgreSQL.

  • 📈 Sub-millisecond Latency - No server-side loops or serialization overhead.
  • 🔒 Transaction Isolation - Every API request runs in its own transaction, ensuring data security.
  • 📦 Rich JSON Outputs - Results are aggregated and returned as standard JSON arrays.

box.title: Reading profiles with query builder chaining:
box.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('profiles')
  .select('id, username, points')
  .eq('status', 'active')
  .gt('points', 100)
  .order('points', { ascending: false })
  .range(0, 9);

box.title: Fetching profiles and their related blog posts without SQL joins:
box.code:
const { data, error } = await supabase
  .from('profiles')
  .select(`
    id,
    username,
    posts (
      title,
      content,
      likes
    )
  `)
  .eq('id', profileId);

box.title: Inserting and updating records with the client:
🕸️ Access your relational data with GraphQL schemas:
list: The `pg_graphql` extension inspects tables and compiles a GraphQL schema automatically.
Allows performing complex queries, page navigation, and mutations on `/graphql` endpoint.
Resolves queries into a single, optimized SQL query plan for minimal database overhead.

box.title: 🚀 Speeding up API endpoints:
list: Use standard indexes (`CREATE INDEX`) on all fields used in client filters (like `eq`, `gt`).
Avoid fetching all fields with `*`; select only required columns to reduce bandwidth.
Implement pagination using `.range(start, end)` to avoid loading massive arrays.

slides.title: Supabase Realtime System
slides.subtitle: Phoenix channels, websocket connections, and WAL replication

slides.title: Inside the Realtime Engine
slides.caption: Instant websocket communication
slides.content:
⚡ Low-latency Data Broadcasting

Supabase Realtime handles real-time sync, client broadcast, and presence status tracking in a distributed Elixir environment.

  • WAL Replication - Listens to Postgres Write-Ahead Log to detect INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE actions.
  • Broadcast Channels - High-speed peer-to-peer data passing (sub-10ms latency).
  • Presence State - Track online participants and connection metadata across sockets.

box.title: Subscribing to real-time insertions on the messages table:
Sending custom, lightweight peer-to-peer messages:
Tracking user online status and synchronizing state:

slides.title: Realtime Architecture Flow
slides.content:
Client Database Change      PostgreSQL Engine (WAL)
          │                      │
          │                      ▼
          │             supabase_realtime (listener)
          │                      │
          ▼                      ▼
  Realtime Server (Phoenix / Elixir Socket Engine)
          │
          ├─► Is request a DB Subscription?
          │     └─► Filter payload using RLS policies per user!
          ├─► Is request a Broadcast?
          │     └─► Instantly relay JSON bytes to channel members.
          └─► Is request a Presence Track?
                └─► Update CRDT and emit sync event.
          │
          ▼
Connected Client WebSockets receive event

note.text: Phoenix cluster allows high concurrency, supporting tens of thousands of connections.

slides.title: Storage & File Management
slides.subtitle: Uploading, serving, and dynamically resizing files

slides.title: Object Storage Architecture
slides.caption: Media management at scale
slides.content:
📁 Scalable Media Storage

Supabase Storage stores binary assets (images, PDFs, videos) inside scalable object storage buckets integrated with Row Level Security.

  • 📦 Bucket Isolation - Logical grouping of assets (e.g. public avatars, private invoices).
  • 🛡️ SQL-based Control - Policies written directly on `storage.objects` table define access rights.
  • 🌐 CDN Caching - Files are cached at edge nodes for high-speed global downloads.

box.title: Uploading file objects using the storage client:
Retrieving file sharing links:
Securing storage files using database rules:
🖼️ Manipulate images on the fly via query parameters:
list: Resizing: Downscale large images dynamically to save user bandwidth.
Cropping: Specify dimensions to generate perfect square thumbnails.
Format Conversion: Convert heavy PNG/JPG files to lightweight WebP formats.
Example: `/storage/v1/render/image/public/photos/beach.jpg?width=300&height=300&resize=contain`

slides.title: Edge Functions & Serverless
slides.subtitle: Running TypeScript serverless logic globally on Deno

slides.title: Serverless Execution at the Edge
slides.caption: Zero cold-starts, global availability
slides.content:
🌐 Deno Serverless Runtime

Supabase Edge Functions are written in TypeScript and executed using Deno, running physically close to your users on the Supabase edge network.

  • ⚡ No Build Step - Imports are managed using ES Modules and import maps without webpack/esbuild.
  • ⏱️ Sub-10ms Startup - Isolated execution envs prevent heavy Node.js lambda cold start delays.
  • 🛡️ Secure Sandbox - Deno runs in a secure sandbox requiring explicit flags to access network/env.

box.title: Deno server-side request listener skeleton:
Connecting to the database with service keys inside Edge Functions:
Calling the Edge Function from user-side applications:
💡 Common server-side applications:
list: Payment Gateway Integrations: Execute Stripe or PayPal webhook verify sequences safely.
Integrations: Connect and communicate with Slack, SendGrid, Twilio, or Discord APIs.
AI Computations: Send prompts to OpenAI GPT-4, evaluate answers, and output responses.
Database Triggers: Call Edge Functions from table hooks to offload long background tasks.

slides.title: AI & Vector Database Capabilities
slides.subtitle: Storing Embeddings and Building Semantic Search Engines with pg_vector

slides.title: AI Capabilities with pg_vector
slides.caption: Postgres as a vector database
slides.content:
🐘 Vector Embeddings in Postgres

Instead of managing a separate database like Pinecone or Milvus, Supabase integrates `pg_vector` directly into PostgreSQL to store and query multidimensional embeddings.

  • 🔢 Vector Columns - Define column types of varying dimension counts (e.g. 1536).
  • 🔍 Math Operators - Calculate Cosine, L2 (Euclidean), and Inner Product distance.
  • 🚀 HNSW Indexes - Fast, approximate nearest neighbor search structures.

box.title: Enabling pg_vector and creating tables to store document embedding data:
Stored procedure to fetch matching documents based on cosine similarity:
box.code:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION match_documents (
  query_embedding VECTOR(1536),
  match_threshold FLOAT,
  match_count INT
)
RETURNS TABLE (id BIGINT, content TEXT, similarity FLOAT) AS $$
  SELECT id, content, 1 - (documents.embedding <=> query_embedding) AS similarity
  FROM documents
  WHERE 1 - (documents.embedding <=> query_embedding) > match_threshold
  ORDER BY documents.embedding <=> query_embedding LIMIT match_count;
$$ LANGUAGE sql STABLE;

box.title: Calling the similarity search function from client applications:
🤖 How RAG fits together with Supabase:
list: User enters search query -> Query text is converted to a vector embedding.
Perform vector search inside Supabase using RPC to find closest document snippets.
Assemble context strings -> Send prompt + context to LLM (GPT-4 or Claude).
LLM responds with highly contextual answers grounded in database facts.

slides.title: Local Development & CLI Workflows
slides.subtitle: Local docker stacks, schema migrations, and deployment strategies

slides.title: The Local-First Workflow
slides.imageAlt: Developer workspace with terminal and editor
slides.caption: Dockerized local development environment
slides.content:
💻 Code Locally, Deploy Confidently

Supabase provides a complete development CLI that builds the entire ecosystem on your local machine using Docker container layers.

  • 🔌 Offline Sandbox - Work on code, DB triggers, and functions without network calls.
  • 📦 Reproducible Setup - Team members run the exact same versions of PostgreSQL, Kong, and GoTrue.
  • 🛠️ Easy Resets - Restore, seed, or wipe database states in seconds.

box.title: Setting up the local environment:
box.code:
# Install Supabase CLI globally via npm
npm install -g supabase-cli

# Initialize project folder structure
supabase init

# Start the local Docker containers
supabase start

box.title: Generating and keeping track of DB schemas:
box.code:
# Create a blank SQL migration template
supabase migration new add_comments_table

# Generate a migration automatically based on local dashboard changes
supabase db diff -f add_ratings_column

# Reset local database and apply all migrations
supabase db reset

box.title: Linking and deploying changes to production projects:
box.code:
# Link local workspace to cloud project reference ID
supabase link --project-ref abcdefghijklmnop

# Push local database migrations to live cloud database
supabase db push

# Deploy edge functions
supabase functions deploy hello-world

box.title: 🐳 Deploying on your own cloud infrastructure:
list: All core binaries (Postgres, Kong, GoTrue, Realtime, Storage) are Dockerized.
Official Docker Compose files and Helm charts are maintained for production hosting.
Helps bypass API limits and storage pricing tiers while keeping ownership of your data.

slides.title: Supabase in 100 Seconds
slides.caption: A lightning-fast summary of Supabase features and benefits

note.text: Supabase gives developers a robust relational database with instant APIs and auth integrations.

slides.question: Which relational database engine lies at the core of every Supabase project?
slides.explanation: Every Supabase project is built on a dedicated PostgreSQL instance, granting you full relational SQL capabilities and superuser access.

slides.question: What happens when Row Level Security (RLS) is enabled on a table, but NO security policies are created?
options: All users can read and write data.
Only anonymous users can read data.
All read and write access is denied by default.
Only administrators can insert new records.
slides.explanation: Enabling Row Level Security closes access by default. Without explicit SQL policies, all database operations on that table are denied.

slides.question: Which Supabase API key should NEVER be exposed in a client application or git repository?
slides.explanation: Both the Service Role Key and Database Password bypass Row Level Security and have admin rights. They must remain strictly confidential.

slides.question: What serverless runtime executes Supabase Edge Functions?
slides.explanation: Edge Functions run on Deno, which supports TypeScript natively, executes inside secure sandboxes, and eliminates serverless cold starts.

box.title: 📋 Summary checklist for building with Supabase:
list: Relational First: Leverage PostgreSQL instead of inventing custom NoSQL wrappers.
Shift Security Left: Secure data inside the database using RLS instead of custom API layers.
Auto APIs: Let PostgREST handle CRUD, saving hundreds of developer hours.
Local Sandbox: Develop locally using the dockerized CLI to safely test schemas and migrations.
